---
title: "`r params$report_title`"
author: "`r params$report_author`"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: false
    code_folding: hide
params:
  # Master Data Path
  data_path: !r here::here("1_data", "master_micro_survey.csv")
  
  # Variables
  entity_var: "district"
  response_var: "parent_trust"
  
  # Policy-Facing Labels
  metric_name: "Trust in Local School Data"

  # --- Report Text (all editable; leave "" to use the smart default) ---
  report_title: "Survey Sentiment: Diverging Bar Chart"
  report_author: "Ibraheem Saqib Ellahi"
  chart_title: ""
  chart_subtitle: ""
  x_label: ""
  y_label: ""
  source_note: "Generated via CERP Analytics Template"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  dev.args = list(bg = "#FAF9F6"),
  fig.path = paste0(here::here("4_output", "figures"), "/"),
  fig.width = 9,
  fig.height = 5, 
  fig.retina = 3
)
```

```{r cerp-style, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
cat("<style>", readr::read_file(here::here("2_R", "cerp_style.css")), "</style>")
```

```{r load-and-prep}
library(tidyverse)

source(here::here("2_R", "2.5_helpers.R"), local = TRUE)

# Load data (headers/values whitespace-scrubbed) and validate YAML params
raw_data <- cerp_load(params$data_path)
cerp_validate(raw_data, params[grepl("_var$", names(params))])

# ==============================================================================
# DATA SCRUBBING & PROCESSING BLOCK
# ==============================================================================
plot_data <- raw_data %>%
  # 1. Safely drop NAs only for required variables
  drop_na(!!sym(params$entity_var), !!sym(params$response_var)) %>%
  
  # 2. Scrub strings and wrap long Entity labels (like long District names)
  mutate(
    Entity = str_wrap(str_to_title(str_trim(as.character(!!sym(params$entity_var)))), width = 15),
    Clean_Resp = str_trim(as.character(!!sym(params$response_var))),
    
    # 3. Intelligently map messy Likert scale data into strict categories
    Response = case_when(
      str_detect(Clean_Resp, "(?i)Strongly Dis") ~ "Strongly Disagree",
      str_detect(Clean_Resp, "(?i)Dis") & !str_detect(Clean_Resp, "(?i)Strongly") ~ "Disagree",
      str_detect(Clean_Resp, "(?i)Strongly Ag") ~ "Strongly Agree",
      str_detect(Clean_Resp, "(?i)Ag") & !str_detect(Clean_Resp, "(?i)Strongly") ~ "Agree",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_ # Flag unrecognizable garbage data as NA
    )
  ) %>%
  
  # Drop any garbage data that couldn't be mapped to our 4 categories
  drop_na(Response) %>%
  
  # 4. Calculate counts and percentages per Entity
  count(Entity, Response) %>%
  group_by(Entity) %>%
  mutate(
    Pct = n / sum(n),
    # Flip the sign for negative sentiments so they plot to the left of zero
    Plot_Pct = ifelse(str_detect(Response, "Disagree"), -Pct, Pct),
    
    # Lock factor levels so they stack outward from the center zero-line correctly
    Response = factor(Response, levels = c("Strongly Disagree", "Disagree", "Agree", "Strongly Agree"))
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# 5. Calculate the net positive score to sort the Y-axis dynamically
sort_order <- plot_data %>%
  filter(str_detect(Response, "Agree")) %>%
  group_by(Entity) %>%
  summarize(Net_Positive = sum(Pct, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(Net_Positive) %>%
  pull(Entity)

plot_data <- plot_data %>%
  mutate(Entity = factor(Entity, levels = sort_order))
```


```{r plot-diverging}
source(here::here("2_R", "2.0_setup_theme.R"), local = TRUE)

ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Plot_Pct, y = Entity, fill = Response)) +
  
  # Draw the stacked bars
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +
  
  # Drop a heavy vertical line exactly at zero to anchor the eye
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = cerp_cols[["text"]], linewidth = 1) +
  
  # Use a high-contrast diverging color palette. 
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Strongly Disagree" = "#e07a5f", # Terracotta (strong negative)
    "Disagree"          = "#f2cc8f", # Sand (mild negative)
    "Agree"             = "#a8dadc", # Pale teal (mild positive)
    "Strongly Agree"    = "#457b9d"  # Steel blue (strong positive)
  )) +
  
  # Format the X-axis as percentages and strip away the negative signs 
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = function(x) scales::percent(abs(x), accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(-1, 1) # Lock the scale from -100% to +100%
  ) +
  
  theme_cerp() +
  
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(), # Remove horizontal lines to keep bars clean
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold")
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = if (nzchar(params$chart_title)) params$chart_title else (paste("Household Sentiment:", params$metric_name)),
    subtitle = if (nzchar(params$chart_subtitle)) params$chart_subtitle else ("Percentage of respondents leaning negative (left) vs. positive (right)."),
    x = if (nzchar(params$x_label)) params$x_label else ("Percentage of Respondents"),
    y = if (nzchar(params$y_label)) params$y_label else (NULL),
    caption = params$source_note
  )
```
